notes:
Responsive layout: the window now adapts to its width instead of losing your
sidebars, plus an always-on-top Overlay mode for note-taking beside a call
(PRD Addendum 21 §U).

**New**

- **Sidebars adapt to the window width.** When there's room the left sidebar and
  the References panel dock beside the editor; as you narrow the window they
  slide in as overlays instead, and **re-dock automatically when you widen
  again** — resizing no longer closes them and forgets they were open. When
  only one fits, the left sidebar keeps its place and References becomes an
  overlay. (§U2–U6.)
- **References peek when space is tight.** Below the docking width the References
  panel opens as a read-only peek (a full-window cover on a very narrow window);
  widen the window to edit a reference card again. (§U6.5.)
- **The tab strip adapts too.** When tabs overflow, a new chevron opens a list of
  every open tab — searchable once you have a lot. In a very narrow window the
  strip collapses to a single `current tab ▾` dropdown. (§U8.)
- **Overlay mode — Always on Top (`⌥⌘T`).** Pin the window above other apps for
  quick capture beside a video call; also in Cmd+K and the Window menu. The app
  now also **remembers its window size** between launches. (§U7, §U11.)

**Under the hood**

- Sidebar *presentation* (docked · overlay · full-window cover) is derived from
  the available width on every render and kept separate from your open/closed
  *intent*, so width changes can never destroy state (§U6.3). Always-on-top is
  behind the `overlay_mode` rollout flag; window size/position persist via the
  window-state plugin, and the minimum window width drops to 300px for capture.
